stations.name: Park Road railway station
stations.walk_minutes_note: Closest station for southern East Brisbane streets.

ferry.note: Mowbray Park CityCat terminal is accessible from much of East Brisbane.

buses.note: Bus routes serve East Brisbane via Stanley Street, Vulture Street, and Wellington Road.

driving.motorway_access: Story Bridge and Pacific Motorway access for the CBD and southern travel.
driving.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 5 to 10 minutes to Brisbane CBD off-peak.

secondary_state.name: Coorparoo Secondary College or Brisbane State High catchment
secondary_state.note: Catchment varies by street and Brisbane State High is one of the most-checked catchments by buyers.

lifestyle.shops_dining_note: East Brisbane has small dining clusters along Stanley Street and around the Heidelberg Street and Vulture Street corners. Woolloongabba and South Brisbane precincts are a short walk or drive for the larger food and culture experience.

pockets.name: Mowbray Park / riverside
pockets.character: Streets close to the river and the parkland. Premium pocket with character queenslanders.

pockets.name: Heath Park area
pockets.character: Family streets with established character stock around the park.

pockets.name: Stanley Street corridor
pockets.character: Closer to the arterial. Mix of stock types and a different price profile.

buyer_profile.note: Buyers who want to live within walking or short driving distance of the CBD. East Brisbane is one of the closest inner-east options.
Parents targeting the BSHS catchment for parts of the suburb. The catchment access is a major buyer driver.
Buyers attracted to Mowbray Park, the river access, and the inner-CBD location.
